text: **Anna:** Hmm.
**Anna:** Oh, {important|重要な} meeting.
**Anna:** Everyone must come.
**Anna:** Well, back to work.
**Anna:** And my {boss|上司} has called a meeting.
**Anna:** I wonder what it's about.
**Anna:** Maybe we're all getting {raises|昇給/給料上げ}.
**Anna:** Oh, there's Jonathan.
**Anna:** Maybe he knows.
**Professor Bot:** Hi, I'm Professor Bott.
**Professor Bot:** This video is all about work.
**Professor Bot:** People talk about {jobs|仕事} and things that happen at work.
**Professor Bot:** Your {assignment|課題/任務}.
**Professor Bot:** is to find as many work words as you can.
**Professor Bot:** Don't worry, I'll help
**Anna:** So Jonathan, what do you think today's meeting is about?
**Anna:** The email sounded important.
**Jonathan:** Well, I have heard people in the building talking about {budget cuts|予算削減}.
**Anna:** Oh budget cuts?
**Anna:** Wait, who has been talking about budget cuts?
**Anna:** Mark in {accounting|経理部}.
**Anna:** That's one person.
**Anna:** That's not people.
**Jonathan:** I know
**Jonathan:** But Mark knows everything that goes on at the {studio|スタジオ}.
**Anna:** That is true.
**Anna:** But wait
**Anna:** Look at us.
**Anna:** We shouldn't {gossip|うわさ話}.
**Anna:** That's how rumors start.
**Anna:** We'll just go to the {meeting|会議|/ˈmiːtɪŋ/|Let's have a meeting tomorrow.} and see what happens.
**Jonathan:** Yeah, you're right.
**Jonathan:** No more talk about {budget cuts|予算削減|/ˈbʌdʒɪt kʌts/|Budget cuts affect many employees.}
**Anna:** Budget cuts?
**Amelia:** Oh no.
**Amelia:** I just heard Anna and Jonathan talking about {budget cuts|予算削減|/ˈbʌdʒɪt kʌts/|Budget cuts affect many employees.}
**Kaveh:** That must be what the meeting is about.
**Kaveh:** When their {budget cuts|予算削減|/ˈbʌdʒɪt kʌts/|Budget cuts affect many employees.}, people lose their jobs.
**Amelia:** Not you
**Amelia:** You're a good {reporter|記者|/rɪˈpɔːrtər/|The reporter wrote an article.}
**Kaveh:** And it's not you.
**Kaveh:** You've done such a great job as a {videographer|ビデオカメラマン|/ˌvɪdiˈɑːɡrəfər/|A videographer records events.}
**Amelia:** I I can't go back to being a {detective|探偵|/dɪˈtɛktɪv/|The detective solved the case.}
**Kaveh:** {Criminals|犯罪者|/ˈkrɪmɪnəlz/|Criminals break the law.} scare me!
**Kaveh:** And I can't go back to being a {teacher|教師|/ˈtiːtʃər/|The teacher explains the lesson.}
**Kaveh:** High school kids scare me.
**Professor Bot:** Did you hear any work words?
**Professor Bot:** They talked about a lot of jobs.
**Professor Bot:** You can make the name of a job by adding an ending to a verb.
**Professor Bot:** Kaveh {reports|報告する|/rɪˈpɔːrts/|He reports to his manager.}
**Professor Bot:** So he's a reporter.
**Professor Bot:** Amelia used to {detect|探知する|/dɪˈtɛkt/|Detect the problem quickly.}, so she was a detective.
**Professor Bot:** If you {teach|教える|/tiːtʃ/|Teach students English.}, you're a teacher and a videographer.
**Professor Bot:** Uh, I think you understand.
**Professor Bot:** Back to the story.
**Kaveh:** Penelope, have you heard the news?
**Kaveh:** Today's meeting is about {budget cuts|予算削減|/ˈbʌdʒɪt kʌts/|Budget cuts affect many employees.}
**Penelope:** That's awful.
**Penelope:** What should I do?
**Anna:** {Update|更新する|/ˈʌpdeit/|Update your computer software.} your resume.
**Anna:** I've already {updated|更新した/アップデートした} mine.
**Professor Bot:** I'll see you at the meeting.
**Anna:** Hello everyone!
**Anna:** {Quiet|静かに} please.
**Anna:** This meeting won't take long.
**Professor Bot:** Then you can all {leave|去る/退出する}.
**Anna:** What is wrong with everyone?
**Anna:** You look like I'm going to {fire|解雇する} you.
**Anna:** Seriously, what is wrong with everyone
**Anna:** Excuse me, Ms.
**Anna:** Weaver.
**Anna:** Yes, I'm gonna well everyone has been {worrying|心配している} about
**Anna:** You know, we've been {worrying|心配している} that you're going to fire us.
**Professor Bot:** I'm not going to fire any of you.
**Jonathan:** No.
**Anna:** The reason for this meeting is to tell you what a good job you've been doing.
**Anna:** And give out new {assignments|課題/仕事}.
**Anna:** {Budget|予算} cuts have been happening.
**Anna:** But only one person has lost their job.
**Anna:** Mark, in {accounting|会計}?
**Anna:** Now, let's talk about those new assignments.
**Professor Bot:** Miss Weaver's team is doing a good job.
**Professor Bot:** How did you do?
